findings.detail: 6 nodes (within 3–8). Bold labels all ≤ 4 words: 'Low Temperature', 'Neutral Temperature', 'High Temperature', 'Reliable', 'Balanced', 'Varied'. All six labels trace directly to corpus §5 Core Concepts (low/neutral/high temperature rows in the worked table; reliable/balanced/varied from the key contrast summary). No bare unexplained acronyms. Governing concept 'Temperature controls how spread out the probability distribution is' lives in the title: config field, not as a ghost node. Three parallel independent chains (LOW→R_LOW, MID→R_MID, HIGH→R_HIGH) match the side-by-side comparison intent — no branching off a single linear path. Classdefs use project palette correctly: sharp=blue (#a5d8ff), neutral=purple (#d0bfff), flat=yellow (#fff3bf), done=green (#b2f2bb). Subtitles use 12px span (above the 11px floor).

flowchart LR chosen for three parallel left-to-right pairs. With no subgraph grouping, Mermaid stacks the three chains as three rows × two columns — exactly the side-by-side panel effect the intent requests. No edge crossings expected since the three chains are independent.
